vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-2174: bug(flywheel): intermittently idles (asks a question / drops ScheduleWakeup) instead of ticking

plan.title: bug(flywheel): intermittently idles (asks a question / drops ScheduleWakeup) instead of ticking
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: The singleton flywheel orchestrator (opus/claude-code) intermittently drops its ScheduleWakeup self-schedule or emits a blocking AskUserQuestion and then sits idle at the prompt (RUN-37: 46 min idle), violating roles/flywheel.md:111 (never block on operator) and :121 (ScheduleWakeup every tick). Recovery exists but is weak: the deacon stage-1/2 idle nudge tells the agent to 'emit a status or pause' rather than to run a real tick, and the terminal relaunch fires at the shared 90-minute threshold — far too late for a sub-minute-tick singleton that is the pipeline's last stand.
narratives.Proposal: Two recovery-side changes in src/lib/cloister. (a) Rewrite the flywheel stage-1 and stage-2 nudge messages in evaluateFlywheelOrchestrator() so they direct the orchestrator to run a FULL tick now (inventory->diagnose->suggest->launch->emit-status) and re-arm ScheduleWakeup(1000s), forbidding ask-and-wait. (b) Add flywheel-specific stage thresholds to StuckRemediationConfig (flywheel_stage1_minutes=20, flywheel_stage2_minutes=24, flywheel_stage3_minutes=28) so the relaunch fires at 28 min instead of the shared 90, without changing work-agent thresholds. Stage 1 stays at 20 min so healthy 1000s (~16.7 min) self-wakes are not pre-empted.
narratives.NonGoals:
- Do NOT attempt a harness-level fix that makes opus/claude-code never drop ScheduleWakeup or never emit a blocking question; this is recovery-side hardening only.
- Do NOT change work-agent stuck-remediation thresholds (20/45/90) or any non-flywheel remediation behavior.
- Do NOT change the flywheel ScheduleWakeup interval (stays 1000s).
- Do NOT remove or raise the relaunch cap (FLYWHEEL_MAX_RESPAWNS=3) or the pause+troubled terminal escalation.
- Do NOT programmatically auto-answer or dismiss the orchestrator's AskUserQuestion modal; relaunch (fresh session) is the recovery.
- Do NOT lower flywheel stage 1 below 20 min.

autoDecisions.summary: Flywheel relaunch threshold set to 28 min.
autoDecisions.rationale: Within the issue's stated 25-30 min target. Fresh-launch is the only reliable recovery for a session wedged at an AskUserQuestion modal that may ignore pasted nudges; 28 min bounds dead-air without thrashing.

autoDecisions.summary: Flywheel stage 1 stays at 20 min; stage 2 at 24 min.
autoDecisions.rationale: A healthy flywheel self-wakes at 1000s (~16.7 min), so 20 min is the first provable missed wake; lowering it pre-empts healthy sleeps. 24 min gives the stage-1 full-tick nudge ~4 min (a tick is sub-minute) to land before escalating.

autoDecisions.summary: Implement via new flywheel-specific config fields rather than overloading the shared stage*_minutes.
autoDecisions.rationale: Keeps work-agent behavior (20/45/90) untouched and makes the flywheel thresholds independently tunable via TOML deep-merge.

items.title: Add flywheel-specific stage thresholds to StuckRemediationConfig

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/config.ts, add three fields to the StuckRemediationConfig interface (after stage3_minutes): flywheel_stage1_minutes, flywheel_stage2_minutes, flywheel_stage3_minutes (all number). In DEFAULT_CLOISTER_CONFIG.stuck_remediation (around line 300-305), add flywheel_stage1_minutes: 20, flywheel_stage2_minutes: 24, flywheel_stage3_minutes: 28 alongside the existing stage1_minutes:20/stage2_minutes:45/stage3_minutes:90. No parse wiring is needed — loadCloisterConfigSync() deep-merges parsed TOML over defaults. Add coverage in src/lib/cloister/__tests__/config.test.ts: (1) the default-config assertion includes the three new fields with 20/24/28; (2) a TOML-override case (e.g. 'flywheel_stage3_minutes = 30') deep-merges over the default while leaving the other flywheel fields and the shared stage fields at their defaults.

items.title: The StuckRemediationConfig type accepts flywheel_stage1_minutes, flywheel_stage2_minutes, flywheel_stage3_minutes as number fields (typecheck succeeds for code reading config.flywheel_stage3_minutes)
loadCloisterConfigSync() with no config file returns flywheel_stage1_minutes=20, flywheel_stage2_minutes=24, flywheel_stage3_minutes=28 (asserted by config.test.ts)
A TOML file overriding one flywheel field (e.g. flywheel_stage3_minutes=30) deep-merges so that field returns 30 while the other flywheel fields and shared stage1/2/3_minutes remain at defaults
loadCloisterConfigSync() preserves the shared stage1_minutes=20, stage2_minutes=45, stage3_minutes=90 defaults unchanged
Rewire flywheel idle remediation: full-tick nudges + tighter 28-min relaunch

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/stuck-remediation.ts evaluateFlywheelOrchestrator() (~line 291): change the three threshold comparisons to read config.flywheel_stage3_minutes (relaunch, ~line 330), config.flywheel_stage2_minutes (~line 335), config.flywheel_stage1_minutes (~line 343) instead of the shared config.stage{3,2,1}_minutes. Rewrite the stage-1 message to a hard full-tick directive, e.g.: 'You appear stuck — ${idleMinutes} min since your last tick. Run a FULL flywheel tick NOW: inventory -> diagnose -> suggest -> launch ready work -> `pan flywheel emit-status`. Then call ScheduleWakeup(delaySeconds:1000) to arm the next tick. Do NOT ask the operator a question and do NOT just re-emit a stale status — advance the pipeline.' Rewrite the stage-2 message as an escalated version of the same directive (keep a 'Stage 2' marker and the full-tick + ScheduleWakeup instruction; still no pause-as-primary). Preserve isAgentIdleForNudge(5min) gate, the dead-process self-heal branch, remediateFlywheelOrchestrator()/FLYWHEEL_MAX_RESPAWNS logic, and the transitionAction/log action strings (stage=N issue=FLYWHEEL action=poked/escalated-nudge). Update the flywheel-coverage block in src/lib/cloister/__tests__/stuck-remediation.test.ts to the new thresholds and message substrings, and add an NFR-1 guard test that NO stage fires for a flywheel idle 16-17 min (healthy self-wake window).

items.title: Given a flywheel idle >= flywheel_stage1_minutes (20) and < stage2, when the patrol runs, it calls messageAgent with a message instructing a FULL tick (contains 'inventory' and 'ScheduleWakeup') and emits a stage=1 action=poked log line

Given a flywheel idle >= flywheel_stage2_minutes (24) with prior stage-1 state, the patrol sends the escalated full-tick nudge (contains 'Stage 2' and 'ScheduleWakeup') and emits a stage=2 action=escalated-nudge log line; pauseFlywheel is not called

Given a flywheel idle >= flywheel_stage3_minutes (28) with prior stage-2 state, the patrol kills the session and calls resumeFlywheel exactly once (fresh-launch relaunch 1/3); markAgentTroubled is not called
Given a flywheel idle 16-17 min (within the healthy 1000s self-wake window), no stage fires: messageAgent, resumeFlywheel, and pauseFlywheel are all uncalled (NFR-1)
The work-agent path (evaluateAgent) preserves the shared 20/45/90 thresholds: a work agent idle 50 min emits a stage=2 nudge, unaffected by the flywheel_* fields (FR-4, NFR-2)
Update roles/flywheel.md to describe full-tick nudge + 28-min relaunch

narrative.Action: In roles/flywheel.md, update the periodic-sweep paragraph (around line 121) so the deacon-nudge description matches the new behavior: the deacon's idle patrol now sends a 'run a FULL tick now' directive (not 'emit a status or pause'), and a wedged flywheel is fresh-launched at ~28 min (flywheel-specific), not the shared 90 min. Keep it to a sentence or two; do not restructure the rest of the role file.

items.title: Running `grep -i 'full tick' roles/flywheel.md` returns the updated periodic-sweep line describing the deacon idle nudge as a full-tick directive (inventory->...->emit-status)

Running `grep -iE '28[ -]?min|flywheel-specific' roles/flywheel.md` returns a line stating the wedged-flywheel relaunch fires at the flywheel-specific ~28-min threshold rather than the shared 90-min one
